introduction.tts_missionControlVoice: Welcome back, operative. Before we add new power, we need to check your core system. Six sounds. No guessing. Clean signals only. Let's run a SATPIN diagnostic.
introduction.tts_missionControlVoice_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural"><prosody rate="medium">Welcome back, operative.<break time="250ms"/> Before we add new power, we need to check your core system.<break time="250ms"/> Six sounds.<break time="150ms"/> No guessing.<break time="150ms"/> Clean signals only.<break time="250ms"/> Let's run a <emphasis>SATPIN diagnostic</emphasis>.</prosody></voice></speak>

visual.scene: Command center reactivates. The SATPIN grid appears on screen and each letter pulses softly.
visual.sfx: low system hum with soft pulse tones across S-A-T-P-I-N

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Say the sound for S. No letter name. Just the sound: /sss/.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say the sound for <say-as interpret-as="characters">S</say-as>.<break time="180ms"/> No letter name.<break time="180ms"/> Just the sound: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="s">sss</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch: That's not it. Just air: /sss/. Try again.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's not it.<break time="180ms"/> Just air: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="s">sss</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Try again.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's not it. Just air: /sss/. Try again.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's not it.<break time="180ms"/> Just air: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="s">sss</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Try again.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Say the sound for A. Keep it short: /a/.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say the sound for <say-as interpret-as="characters">A</say-as>.<break time="180ms"/> Keep it short: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="æ">a</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Short vowel with a quick jaw drop.

feedback.tts_exactMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Short and sharp. Good.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_closeMatch: That was too long. Keep it quick: /a/.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That was too long.<break time="180ms"/> Keep it quick: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="æ">a</phoneme>.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That was too long. Keep it quick: /a/.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That was too long.<break time="180ms"/> Keep it quick: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="æ">a</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Say the sound for T. Stop clean: /t/.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say the sound for <say-as interpret-as="characters">T</say-as>.<break time="180ms"/> Stop clean: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="t">t</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Quick tongue tap behind the teeth.
visual.association: Fast tap with no added vowel.

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Stop before the vowel.<break time="180ms"/> Just <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="t">t</phoneme>.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Stop before the vowel.<break time="180ms"/> Just <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="t">t</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Say the sound for P. Air pop only: /p/.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say the sound for <say-as interpret-as="characters">P</say-as>.<break time="180ms"/> Air pop only: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="p">p</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Lips pop open with air only.

feedback.tts_closeMatch: That was voiced. Try again: no voice.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That was voiced.<break time="180ms"/> Try again: no voice.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That was voiced. Try again: no voice.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That was voiced.<break time="180ms"/> Try again: no voice.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Say the sound for I. Keep it short: /i/ like sit.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say the sound for <say-as interpret-as="characters">I</say-as>.<break time="180ms"/> Keep it short: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="ɪ">i</phoneme> like <emphasis>sit</emphasis>.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Too long.<break time="180ms"/> Short <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="ɪ">i</phoneme> like <emphasis>sit</emphasis>.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Too long.<break time="180ms"/> Short <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="ɪ">i</phoneme> like <emphasis>sit</emphasis>.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Say the sound for N. Use your nose: /n/.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say the sound for <say-as interpret-as="characters">N</say-as>.<break time="180ms"/> Use your nose: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="n">n</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Use your nose.<break time="180ms"/> Tongue up.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Use your nose.<break time="180ms"/> Tongue up.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch: Break it down. Sound by sound. Then blend.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Break it down.<break time="180ms"/> Sound by sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: Break it down. Sound by sound. Then blend.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Break it down.<break time="180ms"/> Sound by sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch: Break it down. Sound by sound. Then blend.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Break it down.<break time="180ms"/> Sound by sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: Break it down. Sound by sound. Then blend.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Break it down.<break time="180ms"/> Sound by sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch: Break it down. Sound by sound. Then blend.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Break it down.<break time="180ms"/> Sound by sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: Break it down. Sound by sound. Then blend.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Break it down.<break time="180ms"/> Sound by sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>

introduction.tts_coach: Good. Your base is solid. Today we add four more sounds. These will multiply the number of words you can read. Focus on the sound, not the letter name.
introduction.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Good.<break time="180ms"/> Your base is solid.<break time="250ms"/> Today we add four more sounds.<break time="250ms"/> These will multiply the number of words you can read.<break time="250ms"/> Focus on the sound, not the letter name.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter M makes /mmm/. Lips together. Voice on. You can hold this sound. Listen: /mmm/. Say it: /mmm/.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter <say-as interpret-as="characters">M</say-as> makes <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="m">mmm</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Lips together.<break time="120ms"/> Voice on.<break time="180ms"/> You can hold this sound.<break time="220ms"/> Listen: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="m">mmm</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Say it: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="m">mmm</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Lips closed with a humming voice.

feedback.tts_exactMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Yes.<break time="180ms"/> Feel the hum.<break time="180ms"/> That's <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="m">mmm</phoneme>.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_closeMatch: Lips together. Keep the sound going.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Lips together.<break time="180ms"/> Keep the sound going.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That was <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="n">n</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Close your lips.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter D makes /d/. Tongue taps, then stops. No 'duh'. Just /d/. Listen: /d/. Say it: /d/.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter <say-as interpret-as="characters">D</say-as> makes <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="d">d</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Tongue taps, then stops.<break time="180ms"/> No <say-as interpret-as="characters">d u h</say-as>.<break time="180ms"/> Just <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="d">d</phoneme>.<break time="220ms"/> Listen: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="d">d</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Say it: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="d">d</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Tongue taps behind teeth with quick release.

feedback.tts_closeMatch: You added a vowel. Stop sooner.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">You added a vowel.<break time="180ms"/> Stop sooner.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: Tongue needs to tap the roof.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Tongue needs to tap the roof.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter G makes /g/. Back of your mouth. Strong stop. Listen: /g/. Say it: /g/.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter <say-as interpret-as="characters">G</say-as> makes <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="g">g</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Back of your mouth.<break time="180ms"/> Strong stop.<break time="220ms"/> Listen: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="g">g</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Say it: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="g">g</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Back of tongue lifts toward the throat.

feedback.tts_exactMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Good.<break time="120ms"/> Strong sound.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_closeMatch: That was /d/. Move the sound back.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That was <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="d">d</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Move the sound back.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: Stop the sound. Don't add a vowel.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Stop the sound.<break time="180ms"/> Don't add a vowel.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter O makes /o/. Short sound, like dog. Not 'oh'. Listen: /o/. Say it: /o/.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter <say-as interpret-as="characters">O</say-as> makes <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="ɒ">o</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Short sound, like <emphasis>dog</emphasis>.<break time="180ms"/> Not <say-as interpret-as="characters">o h</say-as>.<break time="220ms"/> Listen: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="ɒ">o</phoneme>.<break time="180ms"/> Say it: <phoneme alphabet="ipa" ph="ɒ">o</phoneme>.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Too long.<break time="180ms"/> Make it quick.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That was <say-as interpret-as="characters">o h</say-as>.<break time="180ms"/> Try again.</voice></speak>

blending_practice.tts_instruction: Now we blend. Old sounds. New sounds. Same process every time. Read: mat.
blending_practice.tts_instruction_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Now we blend.<break time="200ms"/> Old sounds.<break time="120ms"/> New sounds.<break time="120ms"/> Same process every time.<break time="250ms"/> Read: <emphasis>mat</emphasis>.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound.<break time="180ms"/> Then blend.</voice></speak>

word_practice.image: A glowing word card for god

sentence_practice.tts_instruction: These are real instructions. Read it like you would see it in school.
sentence_practice.tts_instruction_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">These are real instructions.<break time="200ms"/> Read it like you would see it in school.</voice></speak>

introduction.tts_coach: Time to check mastery. You'll read mixed words and one sentence using SATPIN and MDGO. Aim for at least eight out of ten to move on.
introduction.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Time to check mastery.<break time="200ms"/> You'll read mixed words and one sentence using <say-as interpret-as="characters">S A T P I N</say-as> and <say-as interpret-as="characters">M D G O</say-as>.<break time="250ms"/> Aim for at least eight out of ten to move on.</voice></speak>

summary.tts_coach: You now know ten sounds: S, A, T, P, I, N, M, D, G, O. That's a big jump in reading power. Next lesson, we finish the basic code. You're building something real.
